_sources.ovx: CBOE CDN OVX_History.csv - D0 uses Feb 27 close (Feb 28 = Saturday); D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend, market closed); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.vxeem: CBOE CDN VXEEM_History.csv - all D0-D9 confirmed from live data; D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.scenarios: D10-D12 confirmed from banker-conflict.json; all prior days estimated from narrative context

days.note: Wed - Trump declares blockade continues until Iran agrees to nuclear deal (CNBC); Brent confirmed $118.03 (+6.2%, CNBC; previously carried $111.16 Apr 28 - corrected); GCC summit Jeddah (Apr 28): Gulf leaders meet first time since war; May 1 War Powers deadline looms; Democrats exploring lawsuit (Time); Senate blocked curbs 52-47; tail risk rises to 50% on Trump blockade-until-nuclear stance

Thu - Brent hits $126 wartime high (CNBC/CNN; highest since Jun 2022) before closing $114.01 (-3.4% from D58; CNBC); WTI $105.07 (Fortune/CNBC); Trump to be briefed on expanded military options against Iran (CNN); market peak fear event; tail risk 55%

Fri - Trump declares Iran hostilities 'terminated' in letter to Congress - bypasses 60-day War Powers deadline; Hegseth: ceasefire 'pauses' clock; Republicans defer; Collins breaks ranks; no AUMF (PBS, CNBC, May 1); Iran submits new proposal via Pakistan: Hormuz reopens if US ends war + lifts blockade; nuclear deferred; Trump 'not satisfied' - 'blast them away'; Brent $108.10 (-2.1% TE basis; TradingEconomics); TTF €46.15 (+0.35%); tail risk 45%

Sat - market closed; ceasefire holds nominally; blockade intact; Iran holds 3 Western vessels (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas, Euphoria); Trump 'not satisfied' with Iran proposal; nuclear stalemate unchanged; scenarios carried from D60

Sun - market closed; Trump announces 'Project Freedom': US Navy to guide ships through Hormuz starting Monday May 4; 15,000 service members, guided-missile destroyers, 100+ aircraft to be deployed (NBC, PBS, Axios, May 3); Iran's Azizi warns it's a ceasefire violation; Trump: 'very positive discussions' with Iran; also says possibility US could restart Iran strikes; Iran reviewing 14-point proposal; scenarios carried

Mon - Project Freedom operational: US CENTCOM deploys guided-missile destroyers, 100+ aircraft, 15,000 service members to Hormuz; Iran warns ceasefire violation but no immediate kinetic response; Trump: 'very positive discussions'; Iran FM reviewing 14-point proposal; Brent $108.12 (0.0%, TradingEconomics morning snapshot; RETROACTIVE: CNN reports US session close $114.4 +5.8% from May 1 $108.10 - consistent with D64 high of $116.55); TTF €45.08 (-2.3%, TradingEconomics); tail risk 45%

Tue - Trump pauses Project Freedom citing 'Great Progress' toward deal; destroyers remain in strait; violence flares: IRGC attacks on UAE and vessels in Hormuz; Hegseth: 'ceasefire is not over'; Brent intraday high $116.55, closes $109.87 (-4% from D63 US session close $114.4; Fortune); tail risk 40% (deal talks initiated but violence intact)

Wed - US/Iran closing in on 14-point MOU: 12-year nuclear moratorium, Hormuz reopening + competing blockades lifted over 30-day talks window (Axios/CNN); WH believes deal near; Brent crashes -7% to $101.27 (CNBC); WTI closes $95.08; markets surge on deal optimism; Pakistan: 'sooner rather than later'; tail risk 30%

Thu - US-Iran fire exchange: IRGC attacks USS Truxtun, Peralta, Mason east of Hormuz; US sinks 7 Iranian boats; each side claims other fired first; ceasefire nominally intact; Iran 'strongly rejected' some MOU terms; Rezaei demands reparations; Trump threatens to restart strikes; Brent $100.06 (-1%, CNBC); TTF €44.84 (+2.13%, TradingEconomics); JKM $16.84 (TradingEconomics); Gold $4,685.37 (-0.13%, Fortune); tail risk 35%

Fri - Brent +1.2% to $101.26 (CNBC; rallied on fire exchange fear after D65 crash); WTI $95.64 (+0.9%); Trump: ceasefire holds, fire exchange was 'love tap'; Iran MOU response expected within 48hrs via Pakistan; Trump: 'very possible we'll make a deal'; Hormuz ~90% closed; US blockade active; tail risk 35%
